story.title: Clean resilience reference
story.summary: The most robust multiplier-based topology. It keeps the same renewable and battery portfolio, then strongly reinforces the fragile southwest, Cochabamba, and eastern relief corridors.
story.where: Uyuni/Punutuma export, Cochabamba 115 kV loop, Carrasco-Villa Tunari path, and Santa Cruz area support.
story.interpretation: Useful as the clean upper-bound design: it removes N-1 overload hours in this simplified lab, but several reinforcements are proxy-like and may represent new circuits or voltage upgrades in reality.

story.title: Economical hardening reference
story.codeMeaning: Southwest backup around 6x, Cochabamba loop around 3x, eastern relief around 2x.
story.summary: A lower-cost multiplier-based hardening option. It reinforces the southwest export backup strongly, the Cochabamba loop moderately, and the eastern relief path lightly.
story.where: Southwest Bolivia around Uyuni, Litio, Punutuma, Atocha, Telamayu and the central Cochabamba loop.
story.interpretation: Good comparison case: it passes the same gates as the clean option, but leaves a small number of overload hours under some stresses.

candidates.name: newline_southwest_plus_coch_loop
candidates.displayName: Southwest export plus Cochabamba new-line loop

story.title: Southwest export plus Cochabamba new-line loop
story.codeMeaning: Explicit new lines for Southwest export plus the Cochabamba loop.
story.summary: The most useful explicit new-line option. It replaces part of the large southwest proxy reinforcement with real candidate corridors and adds new support around the Cochabamba loop.
story.where: New Punutuma-Uyuni and Litio-Punutuma 230 kV corridors, plus Corani/Valle Hermoso/Santa Isabel/San Jose/Arocagua 115 kV loop support.
story.interpretation: Best physical story for a prototype: instead of pretending old corridors become 6x stronger, it says where new paths would probably be needed.

candidates.name: newline_full_with_strong_coch
candidates.displayName: Full new-line package with stronger Cochabamba loop

story.title: Full new-line package with stronger Cochabamba loop
story.codeMeaning: Selected new lines plus stronger Cochabamba loop reinforcement.
story.summary: Combines selected new lines with stronger reinforcement of the Cochabamba loop and eastern path.
story.where: Southwest export, Cochabamba loop, Cotoca/Bélgica, and Carrasco-Villa Tunari.
story.interpretation: Robust but heavier. It passes the full N-1 gate, but with more topology score than the simpler references.

story.title: Parallel Punutuma-Uyuni line
story.summary: Adds one explicit parallel 230 kV line between Punutuma and Uyuni while relying on moderate existing reinforcements elsewhere.
story.where: Southwest export path from Uyuni through Punutuma.
story.interpretation: Tests the simplest direct fix for the Uyuni export bottleneck. It helps, but by itself does not solve all N-1 weaknesses.

story.title: Southwest triangle
story.codeMeaning: New-line triangle around Uyuni, Litio, Punutuma and Torre Huayco.
story.summary: Adds candidate new lines around Uyuni, Litio, Punutuma and Torre Huayco to create a stronger local export triangle.
story.where: Southwest Bolivia near Uyuni, Litio, Punutuma, and Torre Huayco.
story.interpretation: Explores a more meshed southwest export concept. It improves physical interpretability, but still needs central/eastern support.

story.title: Uyuni-Santiváñez backbone
story.codeMeaning: Long new 230 kV backbone from Uyuni toward Santiváñez.
story.summary: Tests a long 230 kV candidate backbone from Uyuni toward Santiváñez, one of the central grid hubs.
story.where: Southwest-to-central backbone from Uyuni to Santiváñez.
story.interpretation: A strategic backbone idea. It is physically intuitive, but long and expensive in the proxy score.

story.title: Uyuni-Sucre backbone
story.codeMeaning: Long new 230 kV backbone from Uyuni toward Sucre.
story.summary: Tests a longer direct 230 kV candidate backbone from Uyuni toward Sucre, plus parallel Punutuma-Uyuni support.
story.where: Southwest-to-central transfer corridor from Uyuni to Sucre.
story.interpretation: A long-corridor concept. Useful for exploration, but it is not the best performer in the current screening run.

story.title: Full modest new-line package
story.codeMeaning: Many new-line candidates with modest existing-line reinforcement.
story.summary: Adds many explicit new-line candidates while keeping existing line reinforcements modest.
story.where: Southwest export, Cochabamba loop, Santa Cruz area and Carrasco-Villa Tunari path.
story.interpretation: Shows that adding many lines is not automatically better; without the right reinforcement balance, operational stresses can still fail.

story.title: Southwest plus eastern relief
story.codeMeaning: Southwest new-line support plus eastern relief near Santa Cruz/Carrasco.
story.summary: Combines southwest new-line support with new candidate relief paths near Cotoca, Bélgica, Virgen de Luján, Carrasco and Villa Tunari.
story.where: Southwest export plus Santa Cruz/eastern transfer corridors.
story.interpretation: Tests whether eastern relief can replace stronger central loop reinforcement. In this run, it was not enough on its own.

branchNames.SBO-MOX115: Central San Borja - San Ignacio de Moxos
branchNames.PIN-PMA069: Parque Industrial - Villa Primero de Mayo
branchNames.SJE-SJS23001: Central San José II - Central San Jose I
